title: lem:fibexists: global fibre-disjointness of the conormal family $\{L_z\}$ unproven

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The construction requires the affine Lagrangian planes $L_z$, $z\in K$, to be **pairwise disjoint on a neighbourhood $
u K$**, defining a projection $
u K	o K$. `lem:fibexists` only argues *contractibility of the choices* stratum by stratum ('the space of admissible $L_z$ along an edge is contractible'; 'extending to the interior is unobstructed because the space of transverse Lagrangian planes is contractible'). Contractibility of the choice space does **not** establish that the chosen affine planes are globally disjoint.

Disjointness is a quantitative transversality / tubular-neighbourhood statement for the *non-smooth* complex $K$ that is never proved. Without it, $
u K	o K$ is not well-defined and Section 4 collapses. Suggested fix: show $
u K$ can be chosen small enough that the $C^1$-varying family $L_z$ has disjoint fibres (implicit-function/open-mapping argument on the smooth model of $K$).
